summary: Small decompilation pass over Game & Watch Judge item code. The PR added bodies and header declarations for it_802C7774, it_802C78B8, and itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim, added required includes, and reordered existing functions to better match binary layout. Bot report recorded score improvements for three unmatched functions and related .sdata/.sdata2 in main/melee/it/items/itgamewatchjudge, but no full matches were claimed in the loaded evidence.

key_files.role: Main item source updated with new decompiled function bodies, include dependencies, and function reordering.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added it_802C7774, it_802C78B8, itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim; moved itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_Destroyed and itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_EvtUnk
pr_body: reordered some funcs to match binary layout too

key_files.role: Header updated with prototypes for newly recovered functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added prototypes for it_802C7774, it_802C78B8, and itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim

what_changed.text: Added it_802C7774, which initializes a SpawnItem for It_Kind_GameWatch_Judge, spawns the item with Item_80268B18, initializes article state, calls it_802C78B8, records debug ownership via db_80225DD8, and calls it_8027CE64 when spawn succeeds.
Added it_802C78B8, which reads the owner and item JObj, gets facing via ftLib_800865C0, obtains a grandchild JObj, and sets Y rotation to M_PI or 0.0f based on facing.

Added itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim, which repeats the facing-based rotation update and returns true when the owner is absent or ftGw_SpecialS_ItemCheckJudgementRemove requests removal, setting the owner judgement flag before returning true when an owner exists.

Moved itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_Destroyed earlier and moved itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_EvtUnk to the end of the file, consistent with the PR body statement about reordering functions to match binary layout.
evidence_refs: pr_body: reordered some funcs to match binary layout too
raw_diff: deletions and re-additions of itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_Destroyed and itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_EvtUnk

what_changed.text: Added includes for math.h, db/db.h, ft/ftlib.h, it/forward.h, and it/it_2725.h to support the newly added bodies.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: include additions at top of itgamewatchjudge.c

what_changed.text: Added public prototypes in itgamewatchjudge.h for it_802C7774, it_802C78B8, and itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim.

smart_moves.text: The PR updated the owning header when adding recoverable function bodies, avoiding source-local hidden declarations.
Function ordering was treated as part of matching work rather than leaving source order inconsistent with binary layout.
evidence_refs: pr_body: reordered some funcs to match binary layout too

smart_moves.text: The code used typed item structures and project helpers such as SpawnItem, GET_ITEM, GET_JOBJ, HSD_JObjSetRotationY, and GameWatch special helpers instead of raw offsets in the shown added bodies.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: bodies of it_802C7774, it_802C78B8, and itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim

decomp_lessons.lesson: When adding recovered item functions, update the item header with the recovered prototypes at the same time so call surfaces reflect the body evidence.
For item translation units, source function order may need to be adjusted to match binary layout; preserve this as a matching concern when adding or moving functions.
evidence_refs: pr_body: reordered some funcs to match binary layout too

naming_conventions.text: Recovered functions with unknown semantics kept address-style names such as it_802C7774 and it_802C78B8, while event/lifecycle functions used existing GameWatch Judge naming such as itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_Destroyed and itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: function definitions and header prototypes

naming_conventions.text: Header parameter names are more semantic than some implementation names for the spawn function: owner_gobj, bone_index, and judge_num correspond to implementation parameters parent_gobj, part, and arg4.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Function reordering was explicitly used to match binary layout.
evidence_refs: pr_body: reordered some funcs to match binary layout too

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: PAD_STACK was present in the newly added it_802C7774 and itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim bodies, but the loaded evidence does not explain why the padding was required beyond the bot score improvements.
evidence_refs: issue_comment: bot report shows score improvements, not full matches

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: The PR used M_PI and HSD_JObjSetRotationY to express facing-dependent JObj rotation instead of manual numeric constants in the shown source.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: include math.h and HSD_JObjSetRotationY(child, M_PI/0.0f)

notes: The diff and changed-file metadata are strong for what files changed and what code was added or moved.
The PR body is brief; it supports only the function-ordering intent, not detailed rationale for each implementation choice.
There were no loaded review comments or reviews, so review_feedback is empty.
The bot report shows progress percentages for unmatched items but does not claim exact matches; this intake does not promote the functions as matched.
PAD_STACK usage is visible but not justified by explicit objdiff or reviewer evidence in the loaded slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Update item headers alongside recovered item function bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2194 added recovered GameWatch Judge item functions in itgamewatchjudge.c and added corresponding prototypes in itgamewatchjudge.h. This supports a local lesson to keep owning headers truthful when function bodies establish signatures.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itgamewatchjudge.c added it_802C7774, it_802C78B8, itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim

accepted_candidate_records.title: Function order can be part of binary-layout matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: The author stated that some functions were reordered to match binary layout, and the diff shows existing functions removed and re-added in different positions. Curators may preserve this as a PR-specific example of source order affecting layout matching.
evidence_refs: pr_body: reordered some funcs to match binary layout too

source_update_candidates.title: Document function-ordering as a matching consideration when binary layout is affected
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2194 provides a small example where the author explicitly reordered functions to match binary layout. Consider whether existing standards should mention source function order/TU layout as part of matching evidence, especially when adding functions to a partially decompiled translation unit.
evidence_refs: pr_body: reordered some funcs to match binary layout too
raw_diff: function bodies moved within itgamewatchjudge.c
source_update_candidates.reason: Current loaded standards discuss section placement, splits, and verification, but this PR specifically highlights function order as binary-layout matching work.

source_update_candidates.title: PAD_STACK examples should require explicit verifier notes
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2194 includes PAD_STACK in two newly added functions while the loaded evidence only shows score improvements, not exact objdiff rationale. Consider adding or reinforcing guidance that PAD_STACK should carry narrow verification evidence or cleanup notes when retained.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists partial score improvements
source_update_candidates.reason: This aligns with the loaded matching-tactics-need-evidence standard; the PR is useful as a handoff example but does not prove a new accepted practice.

rejection_notes: Do not record it_802C7774, it_802C78B8, or itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim as fully matched from this slice; the bot report shows 90.86%, 97.82%, and 95.73% respectively, not 100%.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale or requested changes; no review comments or reviews are loaded.
Do not promote PAD_STACK usage as accepted style from this PR alone; the loaded evidence lacks explicit build/objdiff justification.
Do not infer data-ownership lessons from the .sdata/.sdata2 bot improvements; no data diff or source rationale is included.
